reasoning: The term premium on a 10-year Treasury bond behaves largely as a random walk in the short term, which would imply a base rate close to 50% for an increase over any horizon. However, the FRED statistical tool indicates a 73% probability of an increase by February 8 based on the recent strong upward linear trend driven by high Treasury issuance and resilient economic data. Blending the efficient market hypothesis (50%) with the short-term trending momentum from the statistical tool (~70%), I estimate a 60% probability for the short-term 1-week horizon. For the longer 1-month horizon (March 3), the predictable drift accounts for less variance relative to random shocks (e.g., upcoming employment reports and inflation data), so the probability should mean-revert closer to 50% (54%).
